zh.lifeSummary: 當John James Weeks於1472年6月出生在英國越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾時，他的父親Sir Richard Weeks32歲，母親Lady Alice Kelly Carey35歲。他於1490年與Lady Alice de Bates在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1521年3月11日在他的家鄉享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John James Weeks于1472年6月出生在英国越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾时，他的父亲Sir Richard Weeks32岁，母亲Lady Alice Kelly Carey35岁。他于1490年与Lady Alice de Bates在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1521年3月11日在他的家乡享年48岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Weeks
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian and Swedish   Vik  .

History: This surname was brought to North America independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One of the earliest on record is Leonard Weeks, who emigrated from Somerset, England, to Portsmouth, NH, some time before 1656. — William Weeks, recorded in Edgartown, Martha's Vineyard, MA, from 1653 onward, was probably a cousin or other relative of the John Wickes mentioned at   Wickes  .

variants.name: Wicks
meanings: English (southern England and East Anglia): generally a variant of Wick , Wyke , or the obsolete form Week, with post-medieval excrescent -s. Occasionally from the plural form of Middle English wik(e), late Middle English weke ‘building, especially one where dairy products are made’ (compare Wick ). An alternative Middle English plural form occurs in Wicken .

variants.name: Weekes
meanings: English: variant of   Weeks   or   Wicks  . This form of the surname is frequent in Barbados.

variants.name: Vik
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Alf, Erik, Lars, Lennart, Nels, Nils.

Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads all over Norway and Sweden, so named with Old Norse vík ‘small bay, inlet’. In Swedish it may also be a topographic or ornamental name. Compare Weeks .
Czech: from a short form of the personal name Viktor (see Victor ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When John James Weeks was born in June 1472, in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Richard Weeks, was 32 and his mother, Lady Alice Kelly Carey, was 35. He married Lady Alice de Bates in 1490, in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 11 March 1521, in his hometown, at the age of 48, and was buried in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John James Weeks est né en juin 1472, à Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Weeks, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Kelly Carey, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alice de Bates en 1490, à Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 11 mars 1521, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John James Weeks im Juni 1472 in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Weeks 32 und seine Mutter Lady Alice Kelly Carey 35. Er hat 1490 in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Alice de Bates geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 11. März 1521 im Alter von 48 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John James Weeks è nato il nel giugno 1472 nel Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard Weeks, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alice Kelly Carey, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alice de Bates nel 1490, nel Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 11 marzo 1521, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John James Weeksが1472年6月にイギリスヨークシュアイアホールメ-オン-スプアルディング-モオルで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Weeksは32歳，母Lady Alice Kelly Careyは35歳であった。1490年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールメ-オン-スプアルディング-モオルでLady Alice de Batesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1521年3月11日に故郷で48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John James Weeks(이)가 1472년 6월 영국 요르크실르 홀--스팔딩-모어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Weeks(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Kelly Carey(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀--스팔딩-모어에서 Lady Alice de Bates(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1521년 3월 11일 그의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John James Weeks nasceu em junho de 1472, em Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Weeks, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Kelly Carey, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice de Bates em 1490, em Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 11 de março de 1521, em sua cidade natal, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John James Weeks родился июнь 1472 г. в местности холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Weeks было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Kelly Carey, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice de Bates в 1490 г. в местности холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 11 марта 1521 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John James Weeks nació en junio de 1472, en Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Weeks, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lady Alice Kelly Carey, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Lady Alice de Bates en 1490, en Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 11 de marzo de 1521, en su pueblo, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
